contribution_description:
[CONTRIBUTION EVENT]
- Type: Time (Minutes)
- Amount: 30
- Description: Diagnose and fix $200+ Places API spend caused by missing PLACES_CACHE_PAT in cron workflows
  
  Pull requests (GitHub evidence):
  - https://github.com/TrueSightDAO/go_to_market/pull/99
  
  Details:
  Used Cloud Monitoring API (after enabling Cloud Monitoring + Cloud Billing on get-data-io via Service Usage API + the SA's Owner role) to identify the cost driver: 11,211 google.places.Details.Http calls today (00:00-05:26 UTC) vs ~600 Hit List rows = 18.7 calls per place per day. Cross-checked TrueSightDAO/places-cache repo commit log: 0 commits today, 1 cached record total (Sydney test). Confirms cache infrastructure shipped in PRs #97/#98 was completely bypassed in production. Root cause: none of the three hourly cron workflows (hit_list_enrich_contact, hit_list_research_photo_review, field_agent_location_places_pull) passed PLACES_CACHE_PAT into the Python step's env block. Without the PAT, places_cache writes silently skipped + reads hit GitHub's 60/hour anonymous rate limit → throttled to 403 → cache miss → live API call → no write → next call also misses. Fix: one-line addition to env: block of each of the three .github/workflows YAML files. Verified via grep that no other workflow needs the same fix. Merged PR #99. Expected effect after next cron tick: cache writes start succeeding, repeat place_ids return $0 from CDN-cached GitHub raw, Places spend drops toward 1 call per unique place_id ever instead of ~19 per day.
- Contributor(s): Garyjob <garyjob@gmail.com>
- TDG Issued: 50.00
- Attached Filename: N/A
- Destination Contribution File Location: N/A
